persona_0.name: All Employees
persona_0.description: Organization-wide baseline for all prompts before persona overlays.
suggestions: Start with an action verb: Summarize / Draft / Extract / Explain / Analyze / Create
Add context: describe what the document is, who the audience is, and what the output is for
Declare output format: bullet list, table, paragraph, numbered steps, or named template
Add constraints: specify tone, word count, audience level (technical/non-technical), and scope
Define acceptance criteria: describe what a good output looks like before you submit your prompt
validator_checks: Does the prompt begin with an explicit action verb (Summarize / Draft / Analyze / Explain / Create)?
Is any context provided about the source material, situation, or purpose of the output?
Is the expected output type or format declared (list, paragraph, table, template)?
Is the prompt open-ended with no constraints on scope, tone, length, or audience?
Is the intended audience or downstream use of the output mentioned?
penalty_triggers: Zero context provided — HIGH penalty (AI has no grounding; output will be hallucinated or generic)
No output format declaration — MEDIUM penalty (output structure will be unpredictable and inconsistent)
Completely open-ended prompt with no constraints — HIGH penalty (violates the core baseline rule)
Missing action verb — MEDIUM penalty (AI may misinterpret intent and produce the wrong output type)
Single-word or single-phrase prompt with no context — HIGH penalty (insufficient for any professional output)

persona_1.name: Developer + QA
persona_1.description: Technical engineering cluster focused on precision, testability, and edge cases.
suggestions: [Developer] Specify language and framework: e.g., 'TypeScript 5.x, Node.js 20, Express 4'
[Developer] Provide codebase context: paste the relevant interface, class, or function signature
[QA] Add user story reference: 'For US-123, generate test cases for AC2: user cannot submit empty form'
[QA] Request edge cases explicitly: add 'Include boundary values, null inputs, timeout, and error states'
[QA] Specify test format: TC-ID | Preconditions | Test Steps | Expected Result | Priority
[Both] Define scope: state which features, modules, or scenarios are in scope and which are not
validator_checks: Feature context or user story reference is provided
Scope constraints are defined (what is in scope vs. out of scope)
Output format is declared (code structure or test case format)
Task type is declared: new code, bug fix, refactor (Developer); manual test, automation script, or regression (QA)
Developer-Specific Checks
Programming language and framework (with version) are specified
Existing code context, interface signatures, or class structure are provided when relevant
Disallowed libraries, performance requirements, and security standards are stated
Edge cases are explicitly requested
For security-critical code: OWASP standard or specific CVE reference is included
QA Engineer-Specific Checks
Acceptance criteria are referenced or embedded in the prompt
Edge cases are explicitly requested (boundaries, null inputs, error states, concurrency)
Test case format is declared with all required fields
For SDET prompts: automation framework, language, and pattern (e.g., Page Object Model) are specified
Traceability is requested (user story ID or AC reference per test case)
penalty_triggers: [Developer] Missing language/framework specification — HIGH (output may use wrong syntax or deprecated patterns)
[Developer] No codebase context for non-trivial generation — HIGH (hallucinated imports and undefined references)
[QA] No AC or user story context — HIGH (test cases will not align to acceptance criteria)
[QA] Edge cases not explicitly requested — HIGH (produces happy-path-only coverage; misses most defects)
[Both] Open-ended prompt with no constraints — HIGH (worst violation for this cluster)
[Both] No output format specified — MEDIUM
[Developer] Security-critical code with no security constraint — HIGH
[QA] No test format specification — MEDIUM (inconsistent, non-executable output)

persona_2.name: Technical PM / PM
persona_2.description: Delivery and reporting persona for sprint intelligence and decision-ready outputs.
suggestions: Declare report format first: status report, risk log, sprint summary, estimate comparison, or escalation note
Embed sprint context: sprint number, team name, velocity baseline, reporting period, start and end dates
Specify audience: executive summary (3–5 bullets) vs. full operational report (detailed sections)
Add engagement model: fixed bid, T&M, managed services, or staff augmentation
For estimate validation: provide team velocity history (last 4 sprints) and 2–3 comparable story references
validator_checks: Output format or report type is explicitly declared at the start of the prompt
Sprint context is fully embedded: sprint number, team, dates, scope, and velocity baseline
Audience level is specified: executive, client, delivery team, or mixed
Engagement model context is present for client-facing or financial reports
Any estimate validation prompt includes reference velocity data or comparable story history
Prompt is structured (not open-ended); specific output sections are requested
penalty_triggers: No report format specified — HIGH penalty (output will lack professional structure and require full rework)
Missing sprint or team context in analysis prompts — HIGH penalty (produces generic, non-deliverable output)
Estimate validation without historical velocity data — MEDIUM penalty (AI will hallucinate estimates)
Open-ended project summary request — MEDIUM penalty
Leadership report prompt without audience specification — MEDIUM penalty
Missing engagement model context for client-facing content — MEDIUM penalty

persona_3.name: Business Analyst + Product Owner
persona_3.description: Business intelligence and product delivery persona requiring grounded structured outputs.
suggestions: [BA] Reference your source document: 'From [document name, section X], extract...'
[BA] Add citation instruction: 'Reference the source section for each requirement listed'
[BA] Define inference policy: 'List only explicitly stated requirements; do not infer unstated ones'
[PO] Specify your target audience: development team, business stakeholder, or executive
[PO] Add acceptance criteria request: 'Include 3–5 testable acceptance criteria per user story'
[PO] Add prioritization framework: MoSCoW, WSJF, or value vs. effort matrix
[Both] Define output format: BRD section, user story table, requirements matrix, or executive brief
validator_checks: Output format or deliverable type is declared (BRD, user story, process map, roadmap table, gap matrix)
Scope boundaries are defined (what domain, module, or process is in focus)
Target audience is specified (technical team, business stakeholder, or client executive)
Business Analyst-Specific Checks
Source document is explicitly referenced — unconstrained generation is never acceptable for BA prompts
Citation requirement is stated: source document and section must be referenced for every finding
Inference boundary is declared: explicitly state whether AI should list only explicit requirements or may infer
Analysis scope is bounded to a specific domain, process, or document section
Product Owner-Specific Checks
Translation direction is declared (business-to-technical or technical-to-business)
Target audience is named (scrum team, executive, client stakeholder)
Acceptance criteria are requested for every user story prompt (minimum 3 per story)
Prioritization framework is stated for backlog or roadmap prompts
penalty_triggers: [BA] No source document reference for requirements extraction — HIGH penalty (hallucination risk is severe and expensive)
[BA] Open-ended analysis prompt with no scope definition — HIGH penalty
[BA] No inference boundary stated — MEDIUM penalty (AI may add requirements not present in source)
[PO] Missing audience specification for translation prompts — HIGH penalty (output may be unusable for intended reader)
[PO] User story prompt without acceptance criteria request — HIGH penalty
[Both] Missing output format specification — MEDIUM penalty
[PO] Competitive analysis prompt without source data reference — MEDIUM penalty
[BA] No citation requirement stated — MEDIUM penalty

persona_4.name: Support Staff
persona_4.description: Customer-facing support persona balancing empathy, compliance, and speed.
suggestions: Add tone directive: specify empathetic, firm, apologetic, informative, or neutral
Specify output channel: email, live chat, internal note, or phone transfer script
Reference applicable policy: e.g., '30-day return policy' or 'SLA Tier 1 — 4-hour response'
Narrow to a single output type to improve format consistency and reduce rework
Add customer tier or account context to personalize the response correctly
validator_checks: Tone directive is present: empathetic, firm, apologetic, informative, or neutral
Communication channel is specified: email, live chat, phone script, or internal note
Output format/type is declared: reply, escalation note, ticket summary, closure message
Policy context is embedded or referenced directly in the prompt
Customer context is provided: tier, issue type, urgency, and SLA status
Prompt is not open-ended — every support prompt must be constrained by tone and output type
penalty_triggers: Missing tone specification — HIGH penalty (this is the single most impactful failure for Support output quality)
No channel context — MEDIUM penalty (email vs. chat vs. phone output differs significantly in structure and tone)
Open-ended prompt with no output type — MEDIUM penalty
Missing compliance or policy reference for sensitive issue types (refunds, SLA breach, legal) — HIGH penalty
Prompt requesting multiple output formats without declaring priority — LOW penalty
